question.text: A short call (call writer) position has:

answer.text: Maximum profit limited to premium received and theoretically unlimited loss potential
Unlimited profit potential and limited loss
Both profit and loss limited to the premium
Maximum profit equal to the strike price and maximum loss equal to the premium

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Short Call (Call Writer): - Maximum Profit = Premium received (when option expires worthless, S <= X) - Maximum Loss = UNLIMITED (underlying can rise infinitely) - BEP = Strike + Premium
**The Trap:** Short call is the MIRROR IMAGE of long call. Short call writer hopes the option expires OTM.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Writing naked calls is extremely risky due to unlimited loss potential.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Short call: Max profit = premium received. Max loss = unlimited. Needs margin deposit.
